events.title: Family Movie: GOAT

The story follows Will, a small goat with big dreams who gets a once-in-a-lifetime shot to join the pros and play roarball--a high-intensity, co-ed, full-contact sport dominated by the fastest, fiercest animals in the world. Will's new teammates aren't thrilled about having a little goat on their roster, but Will is determined to revolutionize the sport and prove once and for all that 'smalls can ball! events.title: Explorum Workshop: Plastique naturel / Natural Plastic
events.description: Explorez les propriétés chimiques du lait en le transformant en plastique naturel et écologique. Remontons quelques décennies en arrière, à l'époque précédant l'invention des plastiques industriels, pour comprendre ces premières méthodes de fabrication. Grâce à une technique simple, les enfants façonneront du plastique en boutons, décorations et autres objets. events.description: Dive beneath the waves and learn how author/illustrator Soyeon Kim brings an underwater world to life in her diorama art. Learn about this amazing art form and how you can start making your own diorama at home. Soyeon will share how she uses drawing, colouring, and cutting techniques to assemble layered scenes that show depth, texture, and imagination. events.description: Dive into our waterways and learn how we use water for drinking, sanitation, energy, and more. We talk about Indigenous understandings of water, and how cities and rural areas manage water supply in Canada. This workshop highlights the importance of the hydrological cycle. The hydrological cycle describes the movement and flow of water on Earth, and involves different stages such as evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. Join us to learn all about the journey of the monarch butterfly. Then use your imagination to design and build your own mock butterfly garden station.  Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 6 to 10. Space is limited.

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Out of this World Challenge
events.description: Have you ever imagined what it would be like to travel to outer space? Join us to learn about space and the red planet then design your own spacecraft. Brainstorm creative ways to solve problems you might face on your journey. Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 6-12. Space is limited; first come first serve. No large groups (five or more people).

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: In this 45 minute to 1 hour workshop, participants will learn how to prepare a classic black sugar milk bubble tea, including decorating and mixing techniques, as well as the breakdown of core ingredients. A classic lidded cup and straw will be provided to enjoy the Boba as you go! (*contains dairy) **This program is drop-in , but space is limited. Tickets will be handed out 30 minutes before the program. Participants need to be present to receive a ticket.**

events.title: Magic in the Moonlit Forest Camp
events.description: Step into a world of magic, mystery, and creativity! In this fun and imaginative workshop, kids will paint their very own enchanted forest using bright acrylic paints. They will explore dreamy trees, glowing paths, whimsical animals, and hidden fairy homes while learning simple painting techniques like blending, layering, and texture. Perfect for young artists of all skill levels 8-10 years old.

events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show!

events.title: Anne of the Library-on-the-Hill story and craft
events.description: 'Growing up in the shadow of the Great War, Anne finds comfort in her neighbourhood library, where she loses herself in books, often imagining herself as part of the story. She particularly loves the books of LM Montgomery — and her imagination really takes off when she learns her beloved author is in town.' events.title: Makedo: Teddy Vehicle Design Challenge
events.description: Explore design thinking as kids send their toys for a spin in a tailored cardboard vehicle! Whether they create a car, train, rocket, plane, or something completely new, kids (and toys) will love to go on an imaginative adventure. Bring in your teddies to test out your vehicle and do a photoshoot! Registration required. For ages 6-12. Makedo equipment will not be taken home.

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Ever wondered how light switches work? How an electric motor moves? How a lie detector works? Come join us for a fun-filled afternoon of experiments using the Snap Circuits electronics kits. Ages 6+ **This program is drop-in, but space is limited. Tickets will be handed out 30 minutes before the program. Participants need to be present to receive a ticket.**

events.title: DIY Shrinky film keychain
events.description: Ever wanted to turn your doodles into custom keychains? Now you can! Join our DIY Shrinky Film Keychain workshop and watch your designs magically shrink into durable, custom accessories. Limited spot only!

events.description: Join Magic Mel for an upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience that inspires kids to discover their inner magic! With a mix of comedy, storytelling, and jaw-dropping tricks, this high-energy show turns the audience into Real Magicians by the end! Ages 4+. Drop-in. Large groups must call ahead to attend.

events.title: Summer Wonder: Writing Camp with Melanie Florence
events.description: Join us daily from Monday, August 10 to Wednesday August 12 for a fun and engaging workshop with award-winning author Melanie Florence. Learn techniques for brainstorming and transforming your creative ideas into stories. Explore plotting and characters to improve your writing skills and craft amazing stories of your own. For children Ages 9-12.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! events.title: Astronomy Coding Club for Kids
events.description: The Astronomy Coding Club, offered in partnership with the Dunlap Institute , introduces kids to the basics of Python coding while exploring fascinating astronomical concepts. Participants will use code to model and better understand real space phenomena such as planetary motion and the phases of the Moon.   *No prior coding or astronomy experience required.*  For ages 9-12! DROP-IN!

events.title: Make Your Own Mermaid Slime
events.description: For ages 6 to 12.  Registration opens July 15.   Get ready to mix, stretch, and squish in this hands-on slime-making program where kids will learn the basics of simple science while creating their very own batch of mermaid slime to take home. We’ll explore textures, colors, and fun chemical reactions in a safe and supervised environment. All materials will be provided—just bring your curiosity and be ready to get a little messy! events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Ever wondered how light switches work? How an electric motor moves? How a lie detector works? Come join us for a fun-filled afternoon of experiments using the Snap Circuits electronics kits. Ages 6+ **This program is drop-in, but space is limited. Tickets will be handed out 30 minutes before the program. Participants need to be present to receive a ticket.**

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. events.title: Astronomy Coding Club for Kids
events.description: The Astronomy Coding Club, offered in partnership with the Dunlap Institute , introduces kids to the basics of Python coding while exploring fascinating astronomical concepts. Participants will use code to model and better understand real space phenomena such as planetary motion and the phases of the Moon.   *No prior coding or astronomy experience required.*  For ages 9-12! DROP-IN!

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Robot Mechanisms and Motion. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Story Planet Comic Lab
events.description: The Artists in the Library series comes to Evelyn Gregory Library this Fall!  Led by professional illustrators Hilary Leung and Joseph Bonsu, children will explore the art of comics and build a portfolio of work culminating in a polished long-form comic. The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.title: Lego Builders Club
events.description: LEGO Builders Club is a fun, STEAM based program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Works by Asefeh Katoozian
events.description: Throughout the month of July the works of Asefeh Katoozian are on display at Morningside branch Asefeh is a multidisciplinary artist and architect with an interest in philosophy. They've also learned how to use these tools responsibly, including how to navigate the ethical considerations that come with AI-generated music.

events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle

This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Creating Parts With Machinery This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.
